title: ammi majus var. apiifolium
intro: Ammi majus var. apiifolium, also known as Bishopsweed or False Queen Anne's Lace, is a plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to Eastern North America. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which are attributed to its volatile oils and coumarins. The health benefits of Ammi majus var. apiifolium include its ability to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of menstrual cramps and menopausal hot flashes. Ammi majus var. apiifolium can be prepared into teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use, and is also used in herbal blends to promote overall well-being.
